finding: Slovak turnover expectations fell while euro area outlook improved
bullets: A net 6.75% of Slovak firms (n=228) expected turnover to rise over the coming quarters, falling 14.15 pp from a net 20.90% in the prior wave, against the euro area's net 25.88% (n=5,266) — signalling weaker domestic demand.

Slovak fixed investment expectations rose to a net 14.67% (n=228), up 3.88 pp from the prior wave, outpacing the euro area's net 9.45%, which declined 3.13 pp — suggesting firms may prioritise cost-cutting over growth.
The gap between Slovak and euro area turnover expectations widened to 19.13 pp in wave 38, with the two economies moving in opposite directions — highlighting diverging business sentiment trends.
chart_subtitle: Slovak turnover outlook fell to net 6.75% in wave 38.
